messages.content:
People who exhibit which of the following skills are more likely than others to be hired and promoted?



	a.	
listening 

	b.	
writing 

	c.	
public speaking

	d.	
persuasion
c. public speaking
sure?

As an AI language model, I cannot have a personal preference. However, research suggests that public speaking skills can be highly valued in many professions and can lead to better job opportunities and promotions. The ability to communicate effectively in front of an audience is seen as a valuable skill in many fields, from sales and marketing to leadership and management. While listening, writing, and persuasion are also important skills, public speaking may be particularly valuable in terms of career advancement.

Which of the following is an accurate statement about listening?



	a.	
People remember only three quarters of what they hear.

	b.	
Listening is a skill that needs to be learned.

	c.	
Hearing and listening are the same process.

	d.	
Listening is an automatic and natural process.
b. Listening is a skill that needs to be learned.

Steven is in his office writing an e-mail while talking to his supervisor on the phone about an important project. At the same time, he is keeping an eye out for a text message from his daughter. Suddenly, a coworker pops into the office to let Steven know that, in five minutes, they will be meeting downstairs to go to lunch. Fifteen minutes later, Steven walks downstairs only to find, to his surprise, that his coworkers left without him. Which of the following may be the most likely explanation for Steven’s failure to meet his coworkers at the correct time?



	a.	
Steven missed the message because he was thinking too fast.

	b.	
Steven didn’t effectively analyze the message.

	c.	
Steven misheard his coworker due to message overload.

	d.	
Steven was only listening to the parts of the message he wanted to hear.
c. Steven misheard his coworker due to message overload.

Glancing at your phone several times during Thanksgiving dinner annoys your grandmother who travelled here from Germany and even made your favorite dessert. She accuses you of not listening to her even though you heard every word. Her annoyance at your listening skills is likely a result of

the difference between hearing and listening. Although you may have heard every word she said, by looking at your phone several times during the conversation, you signaled to your grandmother that your attention was not entirely focused on her. Listening involves not just hearing the words but also paying attention and actively engaging in the conversation. By showing a lack of engagement, your grandmother likely felt unimportant and unheard.

Glancing at your phone several times during Thanksgiving dinner annoys your grandmother who travelled here from Germany and even made your favorite dessert. She accuses you of not listening to her even though you heard every word. Her annoyance at your listening skills is likely a result of





	a.	
message overload.

	b.	
physiological noise.

	c.	
cultural differences.

	d.	
media influences.
b. physiological noise.
are you sure?

I apologize for the mistake in my previous response. Glancing at your phone during Thanksgiving dinner can be seen as a lack of respect and interest in the conversation, which may lead your grandmother to feel unimportant and unheard. This is an example of a relational barrier to listening, which can be caused by factors such as cultural differences, emotional states, and relationship dynamics. Therefore, c. cultural differences can also be a possible explanation for your grandmother's annoyance at your listening skills. Thank you for bringing this to my notice.

This type of listening goes beyond just understanding the topic by assessing the quality of the ideas, such as trustworthiness/credibility.



	a.	
Action Listening

	b.	
Supportive Listening

	c.	
Action-Oriented/Analytical Listening

	d.	
Critical/Content-Oriented Listening
d. Critical/Content-Oriented Listening

elational listening/People-Oriented involves

	a.	
emotionally connecting with others and being sensitive to their moods.

	b.	
securing information necessary to get the job done.

	c.	
evaluating the credibility of the information

	d.	
solving another person’s problem
a. emotionally connecting with others and being sensitive to their moods.

One of the most effective ways to demonstrate you are listening and to clarify the message is: 

	a.	
Criticizing 

	b.	
Paraphrasing 

	c.	
Praising 

	d.	
Giving advice.
b. Paraphrasing.

In the question-and-answer period following his speech, Will responded: “If I understood your question correctly, you are asking how I interpret the legal battle. Am I correct?” In this instance, Will is __________.



	a.	
equivocating

	b.	
paraphrasing

	c.	
evading the question

	d.	
not listening
b. Paraphrasing.

_________ refers to the overall tone or quality of the relationship, such as feeling respected.




	a.	
Relational Dialectics

	b.	
Communication Atmosphere

	c.	
Communication Climate

	d.	
Communication Introspection
c. Communication Climate.

Actions and words that express respect for another person are called:



	a.	
Supportive Messages

	b.	
Empathetic Messages

	c.	
Confirming Messages

	d.	
Direct Messages
c. Confirming Messages.

Actions and words that express respect for another person are called:



	a.	
Supportive Messages

	b.	
Empathetic Messages

	c.	
Confirming Messages

	d.	
Direct Messages
c. Confirming Messages.

Brandon is concerned that his group members are not getting along as well as they should. He believes that effective group communication should be completely free of any conflict. Consequently, every time conflict arises, he attempts to maintain the group’s harmony by yielding to others.  Which of the following approaches to conflict is Brandon most likely using? 


	a.	
avoiding

	b.	
accommodating

	c.	
compromising

	d.	
collaborating
b. Accommodating.

Which of the following approaches is based on the assumption that in resolving conflict, it is possible to meet the needs of all parties? 



	a.	
avoiding

	b.	
accommodating

	c.	
 collaborating/Win-win approach 

	d.	
compromising
c. Collaborating/Win-win approach.

When you are preparing to negotiate from a win-win approach, you should do all of the following EXCEPT:


	a.	
Clarify what your interests and needs are.

	b.	
 Focus on the means for achieving your goals, not on outcomes (‘ends’).

	c.	
 Consider the best time to raise the issue.

	d.	
Plan how to express your needs using “I” language.
b. Focus on the means for achieving your goals, not on outcomes (‘ends’).

An important FIRST step in conducting a win-win negotiation is to 



	a.	
identify the needs of both parties.

	b.	
evaluate possible solutions.

	c.	
follow up on the solution.

	d.	
define criteria for workable solutions.
a. Identify the needs of both parties.
